q: Which comparison between innate and adaptive immunity is correct?
options: Innate immunity is slower and more specific than adaptive immunity
Adaptive immunity responds immediately to all pathogens without prior exposure; innate requires prior infection
Innate immunity is rapid and non-specific; adaptive immunity is slower but antigen-specific and generates memory
Both systems produce antibodies, but innate antibodies are weaker than adaptive antibodies
explanation: Innate immunity (phagocytes, inflammation) acts within minutes but targets any foreign material non-specifically; adaptive immunity (B and T lymphocytes) takes days but is highly specific and creates immunological memory.
